ORDER SUSTAINING DEMURRER OF JUDY EASHMAN BOWER TO CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S VERIFIED CROSS-COMPLAINT WITH 15 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND
MINI MINUTES FOR APR-18-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS DEFENDANT JUDY EASHMAN-BOWERS' DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 15 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS FAILED TO PLEAD SUFFICIENT FACTS TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL, INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION, SERVICES HAD AND RECEIVED, AND QUANTUM MERUIT AS TO CROSS-DEFENDANT EASHMAN-BOWERS. CROSS-COMPLAINANT IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND, IF HE CAN IN GOOD FAITH, FACTS SPECIFIC TO THIS CROSS-DEFENDANT. THE COURT NOTES THAT CROSS-COMPLAINANT'S OPPOSITION EXCEEDS THE ALLOWED PAGE LENGTH WITHOUT HAVING SOUGHT PERMISSION FROM THE COURT, IN VIOLATION OF CRC 3.1113(D). FURTHER, THE COURT DOES NOT CONSIDER A DECLARATION WITH FACTS IN RULING ON A DEMURRER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF YVONNE EASHMAN-SEWELL'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE IS DENIED. CROSS-DEFENDANT YVONNE EASHMAN-SEWELL HAS GENERALLY APPEARED IN THIS ACTION BY FILING A DEMURRER. THEREFORE, IT IS TOO LATE TO FILE A MOTION TO QUASH. (SEE CAL. CODE OF CIV. PROC. SEC. 418.10(E)(3).) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-06-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302,CROSS DEFENDANT JUDY EASHMAN-BOWERS' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION, CROSS-COMPLAINANT ALLEGES FACTS WITH THE REQUISITE SPECIFICITY TO STATE A CLAIM FOR INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION. (SEE STANSFIELD V. STARKEY (1990) 220 CAL.APP.3D 59, 73.) AS TO THE DEMURRER ON THE GROUND THAT THE ENTIRE SECOND AMENDED VERIFIED CROSS-COMPLAINT ("SACC") IS VAGUE BECAUSE THE EXHIBIT WAS NOT ATTACHED TO THE COPY SERVED ON MOVING PARTY, OVERRULED AS MOOT. CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS SERVED ANOTHER COPY OF THE SACC ON COUNSEL FOR MOVING PARTY WITH THE EXHIBIT ATTACHED. IN THE FUTURE, COUNSEL/SELF REPRESENTED PARTY SHOULD CONTACT EACH OTHER DIRECTLY TO ADDRESS AND RESOLVE, IF POSSIBLE, APPARENT ERRORS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT JUDY EASHMAN-BOWERS' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS Overruled. As to the third cause of action, Cross-Complainant alleges facts with the requisite specificity to state a claim for intentional misrepresentation. (See Stansfield v. Starkey (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 59, 73.) As to the demurrer on the ground that the entire Second Amended Verified Cross-Complaint ("SACC") is vague because the exhibit was not attached to the copy served on moving party, overruled as moot. Cross-Complainant has served another copy of the SACC on counsel for moving party with the exhibit attached. In the future, counsel/self represented party should contact each other directly to address and resolve, if possible, apparent errors. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Mtn To Quash Service
Off Calendar Dec-31-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF YVONNE EASHMAN-SEWELL'S Motion To Quash Service is Denied. Cross-Defendant Yvonne Eashman-Sewell has generally appeared in this action by filing a demurrer. Therefore, it is too late to file a motion to quash. (See Cal. Code of Civ. Proc. Sec. 418.10(e)(3).) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-01-2013 Continued To Dec-31-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. AMANDA L. EBEY, TEL. NO. 989-8070 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT JUDY EASHMAN-BOWERS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is granted with 10 days leave to amend as to the third cause of action for fraud, and overruled as to the second, fourth, and fifth causes of action. Martin's request for judicial notice is granted. As to the third cause of action for fraud, the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend for Martin to allege facts, if Cross-Complainant can do so in good faith, with sufficient specificity as to moving Cross-Defendant. (See Stansfield v. Starkey (1990) 220 Cal. App. 3d 59, 73 ("particularity requirement necessitates pleading facts which 'show how, when, where, to whom and by what means the representations were tendered'") (emphasis in original).) The demurrers as to the second, fourth, and fifth causes of action are overruled; the issues raised in the demurrer are factual disputes that cannot be determined on demurrer. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: AMANDA EBEY, ESQ., 415.989.8070, FOR PLAINTIFF YVONNE EASHMAN-SEWELL; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT JUDY EASHMAN-BOWERS' DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained with 15 days leave to amend. Cross-Complainant has failed to plead sufficient facts to state a cause of action for promissory estoppel, intentional misrepresentation, services had and received, and quantum meruit as to Cross-Defendant Eashman-Bowers. Cross-Complainant is given leave to amend, if he can in good faith, facts specific to this Cross-Defendant. The Court notes that Cross-Complainant's opposition exceeds the allowed page length without having sought permission from the Court, in violation of CRC 3.1113(d). Further, the Court does not consider a declaration with facts in ruling on a demurrer. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
